'''Scott Byron Grix''' (born 1 May 1984) is a professional [[rugby league]] coach who is an assistant coach at the [[Leeds Rhinos]] in the [[Super League]] and a former [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|fb}} or {{rlp|so}} for the [[Halifax Panthers]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

He previously played for the [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster Dragons]], [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]], [[Leigh Centurions]] and the [[Widnes Vikings]] in [[RFL Championship#2002–2008: National Leagues|National League One]]. Grix also played for the [[Limoux Grizzlies]] in the [[Elite One Championship]], and [[Wakefield Trinity]] in the [[Super League]] in two separate spells. He also played for the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in two separate spells in the top flight, and spent time on loan from [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] at [[Halifax RLFC|Halifax]] in the [[2019 RFL Championship|Championship]].

On 22 September 2021 he announced his retirement at the age of 37.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=22 September 2021|accessdate=27 September 2021|title=Scott Grix calls time on his career|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/scott-grix-calls-time-on-his-career/}}</ref>

==Background==
Grix was born in [[Halifax, West Yorkshire]], England.

==International career==
Grix is an [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] international, making his début in 2006 against [[Russia national rugby league team|Russia]].<ref name="Scott Grix">{{cite web|url=http://www.widnesvikings.co.uk/players.php?id=11|title=Scott Grix|publisher=Widnes|date=20 July 2007|accessdate=20 July 2007|archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070927201412/http://www.widnesvikings.co.uk/players.php?id=11 <!-- Bot retrieved archive -->|archivedate = 27 September 2007}}</ref> He was named as captain of the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup squads#Ireland|Ireland]] squad for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]], and was one of his countries better players in the tournament.<ref name="Cassidy included in Ireland squad">{{cite web|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/7657596.stm|title=Cassidy included in Ireland squad|publisher=BBC|date=7 October 2008|accessdate=7 October 2008}}</ref>

He later relinquished the captaincy to [[Liam Finn (rugby league)|Liam Finn]], however, he continued to feature for Ireland and was named in their squad for the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]].

In October and November 2015, Grix played in the [[2015 European Cup]].

In 2016 he was called up to the [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] squad for the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup qualification – Europe#Group B|2017 Rugby League World Cup European Pool B qualifiers]].<ref name="Warrington Wolves trio named in Ireland squad for World Cup qualifiers">{{cite web|url=http://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/14763089.Warrington_Wolves_trio_named_in_Ireland_squad_for_World_Cup_qualifiers|title=Warrington Wolves trio named in Ireland squad for World Cup qualifiers|publisher=Warrington Guardian|date=24 September 2016|accessdate=26 September 2016}}</ref>

==Personal life==
He is the older brother of fellow professional [[rugby league]] footballer [[Simon Grix]], and cousin of professional association (soccer) footballer [[Michael Collins (footballer, born 1986)|Michael Collins]].

Grix runs a strength and conditioning facility in [[Halifax, West Yorkshire|Halifax.]]<ref>{{cite web |title=Elite Strength & Conditioning - Brought to you by Rugby League Player Scott Grix |url=https://elitesandchalifax.co.uk/#tmup=/p/3472723-elite-strength-conditioni/ |access-date=19 July 2022}}</ref>
